title: Update the version of conmon used in test
body:
Also start using podmin in /usr/libexec/podman rather then crio.

Signed-off-by: Daniel J Walsh <dwalsh@redhat.com>

comments.body: LGTM once the test suite comes back up
@mheon Seems like latest conmon and your sig_proxy tests are not working.  Any ideas?
@rhatdan Looks like `podman kill -s 9 $containername` is giving exit code 137 instead of 0. Not sure why
Wait, nevermind, I think the `podman kill` is completing successfully, it's the main container that throws an error.

@rhatdan That tests looks kind of dodgy - the FIFO bash should be trying to access isn't being created. I think the conmon fixes from @giuseppe around exit codes might have exposed a problem we might not have been seeing before.
@mheon @rhatdan yes correct.  conmon now returns 128+SIGNAL when the process is signaled, so the 137 exit code is expected in the test that sends a SIGKILL: 128+9
Alright. Should be sufficient to set 137 as the expected exit code.

:pushpin: Commit 003bd3e has been approved by `baude`

<!-- @rh-atomic-bot r=baude 003bd3ea98fd8e633d9f3bb2391fe3ed5cf937db -->
